title: semecarpus ater
intro: Semecarpus ater, also known as the Marking Nut tree, is a plant species native to tropical Asia, characterized by its large, flat seeds with a dark brown, glossy shell and a soft, white interior. The bark, leaves, and seeds of Semecarpus ater have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant has various health benefits, such as treating skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory problems, as well as being used as an analgesic and antipyretic. Herbal preparations of Semecarpus ater include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are applied topically to the affected area for relief and treatment.
